P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
(916) 322·5662 
Timothy Cohelan 
Administration 
322·5660 
Cohelan, Khoury & Flaherty 
2540 First Avenue 
San Diego, CA 
92103 
Dear Mr. Cohelan: 
Executive/legal 
322·5901 
Enforcement 
•• 
Statements of Economic Interest 
322-6.« I 
322-6444 
April 18, 1983 
Re: 
Advice No. A-83-078 
Thank you for your request for advice on the application 
of Government Code Sections 87400-87405.1/ 
In your letter, 
you stated that you were a member of the-San Diego Regional 
Coastal Commission from 1977-1981. 
Presently, you are in a 
law firm that represents clients before the Coastal 
Commission. 
One of the firm's clients is the National Bank 
of La Jolla, which is attempting to secure Commission 
approval for the construction of an office building. 
As a 
Commission member, you never voted on anything related to 
the bank's application, but you did participate in the land 
use element of the Local Coastal Program. 
You've asked for 
guidance on the extent to which you are prohibited from 
representing this client before the Commission. 
DISCUSSION 
Section 87401 prohibits a former state administrative 
official, after the termination of his term of office, from 
being compensated for representing any other person (other 
than the State of California) before any court or state 
administrative agency or officer or employee thereof: 
(a) 
By making any formal or informal appearance, 
or by making any oral or written communication with 
the intent to influence, in connection with any 
judicial, quasi-judicial or other proceeding ifi 
1/ Hereinafter all statutory references made are to 
the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. 


Timothy Gohelan 
April 18, 1983 
Page Two 
(b) 
The State of California is a party or has 
a direct and substantial interest; and 
(c) 
If the proceeding is one in which the former 
state administrative official participated.V 
In addition, Section 87402 prohibits a former state 
administrative official, after the termination of his term 
of office, from being compensated for aiding, advising, 
counselling, consulting or assisting in the representation 
of any other person (except the State of California) in any 
proceeding in which the official would be prohibited from 
appearing under Section 87401. 
The phrase "jUdicial, quasi-judicial or other proceeding" 
means any proceeding, application, request for a ruling or 
other determination ... or any other particular matter 
involving a specific party or parties in any court or state 
administrative agency .... " 
(Section 87400 (c) .) 
This 
definition limits the application of Sections 87401 and 
87402 to situations where the state administrative official 
participated in a proceeding3/ on a governmental matter 
affecting a specific party and now plans to work privately 
for compensation on the same matter. 
In your case, you did not participate in any aspect of 
the National Bank's application for a permit and, therefore, 
you are not prohibited from privately representing it on 
this matter. 
In addition, in most cases, the land use 
element of a Local Coastal Program has general application 
and does not affect specific individuals or entities, such 
as the National Bank. 
If, in the present situation, this was 
the case, then your work on the land use element would not 
result in the application of Sections 8740.1 and 87402.!/ 
2/ 
See Section 87400 for the definition of "state 
adminIstrative agency," "state administrative official," 
and "participated." 
3/ 
See the enclosed copy of the Gorman letter, A-30-l0S, 
on the meaning of the word "proceeding." 
4/ 
However, if the land use plan did specifically affect 
the National Bank, the provisions of Sections 87401 and 87402 
apply. 
(See the enclosed copy of the Gorman letter.) 
Please 
note that, in situations where the provisions of Sections 
87401 and 87402 apply, you are not only prevented from repre-
senting an individual or entity before the Coastal Commission, 
but also before any court, state administrative agency, or 
officer or employee thereof. 
(See Section 87401.) 


Timothy Cohelan 
Ap~il 18, 1983 
Page Three 
You stated in your letter that the other two members 
of your firm also represent clients before the Coastal 
Commission. 
If, under Sections 87401 and 87402, you would 
be prohibited from representing a client, you may not 
receive compensation because of your partners' representa-
tion of the client. 
We have previously considered how an 
attorney in a two-person firm can isolate himself from 
involvement in a case being handled by his partner (see 
the enclosed copy of the Zatopa letter, A-82-095). 
I have 
also enclosed a "US Law Week" article discussing isolation 
in larger firms.5/ 
Because the issue of isolation is 
complex, if you are confronted with a specific situation, 
please contact us for advice. 
I hope I have answered all of your questions for you. 
If I can be of any further help, please feel free to contact 
me at 916/322-5901. 
JS~:km 
Enclosures 
~/ Under federal law. 
Very truly yours, 
J!rtf~tha~~~n~~ 
Counsel, Legal Division 


March 14, 1983 
Mr. Dan Stanford 
LA W OFFICE!' OF 
COHEI"AN, KHOCRY & FLAHERTY 
2:'dO FIRST A \"E~TE 
SA~ DIEGO, CALIFORNIA 1)210:3 
(7 III 230-8 H8 
Chairman, Fair Political Practices Commission 
III "K" street 
Sacramento, CA 
Dear Mr. Stanford: 
I am an attorney in private practice with the firm of Cohelan, 
Khoury & Flaherty. 
My law practice takes me, primarily, to court-
rooms, but from time to time we are retained to represent applicants 
before various local and state agencies in land use matters. 
The purpose of 1:his letter is to inquire of the Commission of the 
impact of Govermnent Code Section 87401, if known, on the ability 
of this law firm to represent individuals in front of the State 
Coastal Commission. 
I served on the San Diego Regional Commission 
from 1977 until lts expiration in 1981. 
I recognize that a cursory reading of the above referenced Govern-
ment Code section would preclude me from representing an applicant 
that had previously been before the Commission. 
This clearly will 
not be a circumstance in which this firm will find itself. 
The 
question in this situation seems to relate to the definition of 
"proceeding" and "participated". 
Our firm presently represents the National Bank of La Jolla in its 
attempts to secure local governmental approvals and State Coastal 
Commission approvals of an office building in the La Jolla area of 
San Diego County_ 
Clearly, I have never voted on anything relating 
to this particular application. 
The question would be whether or 
not my early participation in the land use element of the Local 
Coastal Program would be deemed a "proceeding" and/or "participation" 
within the meaning of Government Code Section 87401. 
Your opinion in this matter is 
of this firm and, in particular, 
individuals fUlly and completely 
County. 
to clarify the availability 
the undersigned to represent 
in land use matters in San Diego 
for 
attention to this 
st. 
Ti~thy Co 
an 
CI)HELAN, KHOURY & FLAHERTY

sections.question:
in this situation seems to relate to the definition of 
"proceeding" and "participated". 
Our firm presently represents the National Bank of La Jolla in its 
attempts to secure local governmental approvals and State Coastal 
Commission approvals of an office building in the La Jolla area of 
San Diego County_ 
Clearly, I have never voted on anything relating 
to this particular application. 
The question would be whether or 
not my early participation in the land use element of the Local 
Coastal Program would be deemed a "proceeding" and/or "participation" 
within the meaning of Government Code Section 87401. 
Your opinion in this matter is 
of this firm and, in particular,

sections.conclusion: The requestor is not prohibited from privately representing the National Bank of La Jolla on this matter because: (1) he did not participate in any aspect of the bank's specific permit application; and (2) the land use element of a Local Coastal Program generally has broad application and does not affect specific individuals or entities like the bank, thus Sections 87401 and 87402 do not apply. However, if the land use plan did specifically affect the National Bank, the provisions would apply. Additionally, the requestor cannot receive compensation from his partners' representation of clients if he would be prohibited from representing them himself.

sections.question_synthetic: The requestor seeks clarification on whether his prior participation as a Coastal Commission member in developing the land use element of the Local Coastal Program prohibits him from representing the National Bank of La Jolla before the Commission under Government Code Sections 87401-87402.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concludes that the requestor may represent the National Bank because he did not participate in the bank's specific application proceedings, and general land use planning work typically does not constitute participation in a specific proceeding affecting that party. The restrictions of Sections 87401-87402 apply only when the official participated in a proceeding affecting a specific party that the official now seeks to represent for compensation.
sections.parsing_notes: Mixed formats: ocr, old; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request letter (dated March 14, 1983) and the FPPC response letter (dated April 18, 1983). OCR quality is good with minimal errors. Full Q&C sections present. The advice addresses post-termination representation restrictions and includes discussion of related isolation provisions for law firm partners.

CONCLUSION: The requestor is not prohibited from privately representing the National Bank of La Jolla on this matter because: (1) he did not participate in any aspect of the bank's specific permit application; and (2) the land use element of a Local Coastal Program generally has broad application and does not affect specific individuals or entities like the bank, thus Sections 87401 and 87402 do not apply. However, if the land use plan did specifically affect the National Bank, the provisions would apply. Additionally, the requestor cannot receive compensation from his partners' representation of clients if he would be prohibited from representing them himself.

embedding.summary: FPPC Advice Letter A-83-078 addresses whether a former Coastal Commission member can represent a client before the Commission in a matter where he did not vote but had participated in general land use planning. The FPPC concludes the representation is permitted because the requestor did not participate in the specific proceeding affecting the client and general land use planning does not constitute participation under Government Code Sections 87401-87402.
